title: Neighbor Solicitation handler doesn't check for DAD (unspecified source)
description:
File: rust/lichen-ipv6/src/lib.rs:775-803

When handling Neighbor Solicitation, the code doesn't distinguish between regular NS and Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) NS. Per RFC 4861:

1. When source is unspecified (::), it's a DAD probe
2. DAD response should be sent to all-nodes multicast (ff02::1), not to the source
3. The 'solicited' flag MUST be zero for DAD responses

Current code always responds to ip_header.src with solicited=true:
```rust
let na = NeighborAdvertisement {
    target: *local_addr,
    router: false,
    solicited: true,  // Wrong for DAD
    override_flag: true,
};
let reply_icmp = na.build(local_addr, &ip_header.src);  // Wrong dst for DAD
```

For DAD (src == ::), response should be:
- dst = ff02::1 (all-nodes multicast)
- solicited = false

This could cause DAD to fail or behave incorrectly when integrating with standard IPv6 stacks.
